articles.title: Delhi Hotel Fire: Anger Erupts as Safety Lapses, Locked Exits Linked to 21 Deaths
articles.description: Public outrage is mounting after a devastating hotel fire in South Delhi claimed at least 21 lives, with serious allegations of negligence, safety violations, and administrative failure emerging from the ground. As forensic experts, police teams, and multiple agencies continue their investigation, grieving families and local residents are demanding accountability for what many describe as a preventable tragedy. According to eyewitness accounts, the fire is believed to have started in the basement area, where commercial activities were allegedly operating despite concerns over safety infrastructure. Residents and survivors have questioned the absence of adequate fire-fighting equipment, emergency response systems, and accessible evacuation routes within the building. Several locals claimed that people trapped on upper floors were forced to jump from windows and balconies in desperate attempts to escape the flames. Nearby residents reportedly rushed to help by arranging mattresses and assisting rescue efforts before emergency teams arrived. Witnesses also alleged that one of the building’s exit routes remained inaccessible during the crisis, potentially worsening the situation for those trapped inside. The incident has once again drawn attention to alleged irregularities in commercial establishments operating within densely populated residential zones. Questions are being raised over whether the building possessed valid fire safety clearances, occupancy approvals, and emergency evacuation provisions required under regulations. Families of victims and missing individuals have gathered outside hospitals seeking information, while survivors continue to recount harrowing scenes of panic and chaos. The tragedy has also reignited debate over enforcement failures, with critics asking why violations were not addressed before lives were lost. Authorities have assured a thorough investigation into the cause of the fire, compliance records, and possible negligence by those responsible for operating the establishment. As the probe progresses, demands for strict action and a comprehensive safety audit of similar properties across the city are growing louder. The disaster stands as a grim reminder of the consequences of ignoring basic fire safety standards in high-density urban areas.

articles.title: Breaking News: Alleged Attack on Khan Sir’s Coaching Centre in Patna
articles.description: A major controversy erupted in Patna after an alleged attack on the coaching institute run by popular educator Khan Sir. The incident reportedly took place around 11 p.m. in the Musallahpur area, where a group of 10–12 individuals allegedly vandalized property, damaged banners and posters, and assaulted security personnel. One guard was reportedly injured and hospitalized. Khan Sir claimed that 10–12 rounds of firing were also carried out during the attack and alleged that rival coaching operators were behind the incident because of his low-fee educational model and successful student results. However, police officials stated that no evidence of gunfire has yet been confirmed and that the matter remains under investigation. Security has been increased around the coaching center, and authorities are reviewing CCTV footage to identify the suspects. Police indicated that the dispute may be linked to poster-related issues or rivalry between coaching institutes, but stressed that all claims are being verified. An FIR is being prepared, and strict action has been promised against those involved.
